original_abstract:
POL-index is a new Polish citation database developed by the Centre for Open Science at the Interdisciplinary Centre for Mathematical and Computational Modelling, University of Warsaw. The project was initiated in response to the 2012 evaluation of humanities and social sciences journals by the Polish Ministry of Science and Higher Education, which highlighted a lack of formal impact metrics for domestic "list B" publications. 

The system is designed with a flexible data acquisition model to accommodate the diverse publishing landscape of the humanities and social sciences. While the initial phase relies on journal representatives uploading data via a dedicated XML importer, the long-term objective is to establish a seamless workflow through integration with existing national bibliographic databases. This collaborative approach aims to minimize the administrative burden on editorial offices while ensuring data quality through a transparent, community-driven verification process. 

A key output of the system is the Polish Impact Coefficient (PWW). Unlike traditional metrics, the PWW is tailored to the specific citation patterns of the humanities—accounting for longer citation windows while maintaining focus on a journal's current standing. This coefficient is set to become a formal component of the ministerial journal evaluation process beginning in 2014, providing a more nuanced and data-driven understanding of the scholarly influence of Polish academic journals.
humanized_abstract:
POL-index is a new Polish citation database prepared by the Centre for Open Science at the Interdisciplinary Centre for Mathematical and Computational Modelling of the University of Warsaw. The need for its creation was connected with the assessment of journals made by the Polish Ministry of Science and Higher Education in 2012 for humanities and social sciences, that were on the list B and, therefore, without any formal impact metrics for publications from this group of journals, published in that period.

The data acquisition model for the system is flexible, taking into account the various publication practices within the humanities and social sciences. As a first step, journals' metadata will be provided by journal suppliers in the form of files for automated import into the system via an XML interface. In the long run, however, it is planned to link up to the national bibliographic databases, so that the amount of work for the editorial staff can be kept as low as possible and the validity of the data confirmed via a transparent, community-based model of verification.

One of the main results of the system is the Polish Impact Coefficient (PWW). An explanation of this coefficient and the differences it presents when compared to other metrics can be found in part II. The PWW is adapted to the citation styles of the humanities sector, with a longer look back time, but at the same time takes into account the current situation of the journal. Since 2014, the PWW is to become a formal element of the proceedings of the Ministry of Science and Higher Education evaluation of journals.
